context:
SailAway.com is a start-up company which will allow sailboat owners to offer their boats for rent or charter (the term commonly used in the boating industry). Boat owners all over the world will be able to list their boats for charter, and customers will be able to locate boats that meet their specifications and charter them. 
For boat owners, SailAway will provide the ability to list a boat for charter in a specific location, provide a description of the boat and its amenities, provide pictures of the boat, list charter rates by season, and optionally accept credit cards for deposits and payments through SailAway’s credit card handling services. SailAway will also provide optional boat insurance during the charter period. 
For customers wishing to charter a boat, SailAway will provide the ability to search for boats based on location, size, and amenities. Customers will be able to contact the owners, send deposits and payments by credit card (if the boat owner offers this service), and write a review of the boat after their charter completes. The site will also offer vacation insurance coverage.
If the site does well, SailAway plans to branch out and offer a sister site called MotorAway, a site that will offer charters of motor yachts. 
You are part of the Scrum Team formed for this project. Your Product Owner, Claire Buford, is from the product marketing department. You will be using Scrum, with which you have limited experience, and you have a newly certified Scrum master. 
Beginning four months from now, there will be a major marketing push to get boat owners to list their boats, and to make potential charter customers aware of the site. Claire needs to have at least a basic site up and running by that time.
From the Scrum Master:
“I talked to our project sponsor, the owner of SailAway. Basically what he’s looking for is a website up and running in four months. The website will connect boat owners with customers who want to rent boats. This will include international boat owners and customers. Boat owners will be able to list their boats, including descriptions and photos. Customers will indicate which boats they want and be able to pay. There will have to be some security features and penalties for boats that are returned damaged or not returned at all. 
“The sponsor wants the website fully functional when we go live. You know how sponsors are—they want everything and never want to pay for anything. The budget is going to be very important. We’re going to have to watch costs carefully. I’m thinking we’re going to need contract developers and they’re not cheap. I hope we get a team that can do testing. I think we’re going to need to purchase automated testing software. That’ll take a chunk out of the budget. Anyhow, let’s get the team assembled and get going right away!”
You are a business analyst assigned to the delivery team for the duration of the effort to create the initial Sail Away web site. Because Scrum is still somewhat new to your organization, several team members have voiced their concerns about such things as who has the authority and responsibility to propose changes to features and who ideally should be involved in the change discussions. Also of concern is who will analyze and approve any changes to priorities during the project. Given the organization’s past reliance on requirements documents, you also have an interest in how requirements and changes to them will be handled.
question_text: What is one of the first thing(s) you need to do to ensure a successful outcome?

context:
Willow Brook Fitness
Overview 
Willow Brook Fitness & Health Center is a privately-owned fitness and health franchise business headquartered in the suburbs of a metropolitan city. Willow Brook franchises its fitness and health concept to people who want to add value to people’s lives by offering a casual, yet supportive place to exercise and improve their health and personal lifestyle. Their initial success led them to build four more fitness centers in the metropolitan area. 
Several years ago, they decided to expand by franchising their fitness concept and all their processes. Willow Brook then hired Greg Smith, an experienced sales and marketing professional in the franchise industry. In the past five years, Willow Brook has gone from 20 franchises the first year to over 350 franchises, located in 19 states. However, their growth rate has slowed dramatically the past two years and worse, some franchisees have left the organization. The owners have sponsored an effort to help stop the decline. 
The following table shows the growth data of franchisees over the past 5 years. 
  [HÌNH ẢNH: img_aa27ae06b85175c4.png] 
The “Family” Connection 
At the start of the franchising effort, the owners’ four children joined the company. Lynn is a certified nutritionist who provides the franchisees or their designated nutritionists with nutritional advice and programs. Courtney is a certified fitness instructor and in charge of developing fitness programs and training fitness instructors for all the franchises. Chris became a massage therapist and is now in charge of developing massage services and programs that can be replicated in the franchises. He is also in charge of leading the training effort of massage therapists in the services and processes supported by Willow Brook. Finally, their youngest son, Garrett, became their IT director. He recently graduated from a local University and has assisted the company with all their computer efforts through graduate school. He has a master’s degree in computer science.
Current Situation/Needs 
The current franchise system is not meeting the needs of their franchisees as shown by the following summary compiled by a business analyst assigned to the project. You have now been asked by the project manager for this effort to lead the business analysis work as the senior business analyst. 
• The current system has inconsistencies and redundancies which causes ongoing frustration for the franchisees, their employees, and to the corporate trainers who do the system training. 
• Adding a new franchisee is a complicated and error-prone process that takes up to a week to perform.
• Calls to the customer service desk have increased by fifteen percent in the past nine months, while customer satisfaction has decreased by ten percent. 
• The sponsors are worried that the unique Willow Brook experience will deteriorate for the franchisees and their employees and are concerned about public and industry perception.

context:
Overview
Newbank is a mid-sized regional bank that has had steady but not exceptional growth in the last decade. During this time it concentrated on expanding into new national markets and upgrading its computer systems. More recently, it has seen a sharp rise in customer complaints concerning the amount of paper that is generated and sent to customers, including statements, promotions, tax forms, and other bank communications. 
Current System Limitations
The business case for the initiative cited these major business problems to be addressed: 
• Currently statements can be sent to only one address, and they are addressed to the customer who is designated as the primary customer on the account. This lack of flexibility has been one of the chief sources of customer complaints. 
• Numerous requests have been received to have duplicate statements sent to different customers at different locations. 
• In all of its current systems (checking, savings, loans, etc.), each account at Newbank keeps its own name/address information, which means the same customer information is entered and maintained several times, once for each account. 
• Newbank must separately mail or email monthly statements, end-of-year interest reports, and other official mailings. This issue costs the bank excess postage and other costs, and is annoying to customers. 
The proposed solution for the initiative included consolidating address information into one place for all of a customer’s accounts. It also proposed adding the option of receiving one comprehensive statement for all the accounts in one household but also to send separate statements to account owners who request it. These features gives them a competitive advantage. 
The following information related to handling statements was available for you to review, the first three of which are shortened for brevity: 
CUSTOMERS - 
Newbank has several types of customers, including consumers, commercial, loan, and mortgage. The majority of their customers are consumers, but low interest rates have helped the loan and mortgage business as well. The bank is hoping to increase its commercial customers by 10-15% this year.
ACCOUNTS - 
An account is a vehicle for customers to safely keep money in a financial institution for some purpose, such as: a) the ability to write checks and have them be paid by the bank, b) to accumulate interest and c) to have liquidity of funds. Newbank offers checking, savings, and certificates of deposit.
LOCATIONS - 
Customers may have any number of addresses. Newbank indicates a primary address as a default, but that default can be changed upon customer request, either written or through the website. 
STATEMENTS
A. BUSINESS DEFINITIONS 
• A statement is a document that lists a) details of transactions that occurred during the most recent reporting period and b) summary information, such as totals of transaction types and beginning/ending balances. 
• Synonyms: None
B. BUSINESS RULES 
• An account is reported on at least one statement for free. If a customer desires additional reporting of an account, they can have it done for a fee (defined elsewhere). 
• A statement reports at least one account. 
• A statement is mailed to at least one location. 
• A location can receive several statements. 
C. EXAMPLES 
• Statement A: mailed to home address - Account 501 - joint checking between Bob and Mary No fee, Account 502 - individual checking - Bob No fee 
• Statement B: mailed to Bob's office - Account 502 - individual checking - Bob Fee 
• Statement C: both mailed to Mary's office - Account 504 - individual savings - Mary No fee, Account 504 - duplicate copy Fee

Newbank has several types of customers, including consumers, commercial, loan, and mortgage. The majority of their customers are consumers, but low interest rates have helped the loan and mortgage business as well. The bank is hoping to increase its commercial customers by 10-15% this year.
ACCOUNTS - 
An account is a vehicle for customers to safely keep money in a financial institution for some purpose, such as: a) the ability to write checks and have them be paid by the bank, b) to accumulate interest and c) to have liquidity of funds. Newbank offers checking, savings, and certificates of deposit.
LOCATIONS - 
Customers may have any number of addresses. Newbank indicates a primary address as a default, but that default can be changed upon customer request, either written or through the website. 
STATEMENTS
A. BUSINESS DEFINITIONS 
• A statement is a document that lists a) details of transactions that occurred during the most recent reporting period and b) summary information, such as totals of transaction types and beginning/ending balances. 
• Synonyms: None
B. BUSINESS RULES 
• An account is reported on at least one statement for free. If a customer desires additional reporting of an account, they can have it done for a fee (defined elsewhere). 
• A statement reports at least one account. 
• A statement is mailed to at least one location. 
• A location can receive several statements. 
C. EXAMPLES 
• Statement A: mailed to home address - Account 501 - joint checking between Bob and Mary No fee, Account 502 - individual checking - Bob No fee 
• Statement B: mailed to Bob's office - Account 502 - individual checking - Bob Fee 
• Statement C: both mailed to Mary's office - Account 504 - individual savings - Mary No fee, Account 504 - duplicate copy Fee
question_text: Given the fee structure desired for statements, what would be the preferred way to model or specify them?

context:
Overview
Newbank is a mid-sized regional bank that has had steady but not exceptional growth in the last decade. During this time it concentrated on expanding into new national markets and upgrading its computer systems. More recently, it has seen a sharp rise in customer complaints concerning the amount of paper that is generated and sent to customers, including statements, promotions, tax forms, and other bank communications. 
Current System Limitations
The business case for the initiative cited these major business problems to be addressed: 
• Currently statements can be sent to only one address, and they are addressed to the customer who is designated as the primary customer on the account. This lack of flexibility has been one of the chief sources of customer complaints. 
• Numerous requests have been received to have duplicate statements sent to different customers at different locations. 
• In all of its current systems (checking, savings, loans, etc.), each account at Newbank keeps its own name/address information, which means the same customer information is entered and maintained several times, once for each account. 
• Newbank must separately mail or email monthly statements, end-of-year interest reports, and other official mailings. This issue costs the bank excess postage and other costs, and is annoying to customers. 
The proposed solution for the initiative included consolidating address information into one place for all of a customer’s accounts. It also proposed adding the option of receiving one comprehensive statement for all the accounts in one household but also to send separate statements to account owners who request it. These features gives them a competitive advantage.

context: Overview Belle's Bags is a rapidly growing online seller of handbags and purses, wallets, and accessories. Belle began making handbags as a hobby to sell at local art fairs. They were so popular, that she decided to become an entrepreneur and started selling through the internet. She has become so successful that she employs hundreds of people, including many working at the distribution center (DC) 24/7. Belle's sells to individuals and wholesale to a number of other retailers. Customers may be either individuals or businesses. An individual can also be a business. For example, Marisa Fortes might buy a purse for herself and a variety of product for her retail business. Most of the business customers are boutique retailers. Orders are placed online. There is no limit to the number of line items that can be placed on an order. An order really isn't an order unless at least one item has been ordered. Each line item is associated with a specific order. The Order system checks immediately to see if the item is in stock in the Inventory system. If in stock, the items on the order are reserved and immediately transmitted to the DC, where they are filled and shipped. If any of the items on the order are out of stock, that item is placed in a status of "Open." When the open item(s) is received in the DC, the Order system is updated immediately and the item is filled. The status of each item is updated along the way in a variety of systems, including the Order, Inventory, Accounting, Picking, and Shipping systems. When a customer inquires about the status of an order online, they must enter the order number and then the system checks the status of each order item on the order in each of the systems where it is stored. Once found, the system displays the status of the item. When the customer calls about the status of an order item, the CSR’s must check each system individually. From the CEO, Belle Bright: We're experiencing tremendous growing pains and our slow systems are not keeping up. We do care about customer service. The number of complaints has increased by about 10% over the last two years. Some of the comments on social media can be quite nasty. From the customer service supervisor, Dawn Murphy: Boy do we need new order processing software around here. We have a lot of customer complaints. For example, when customers would like to check their order status, it takes forever because the order system must request information from multiple systems, some of which are very slow. Sigh. From the VP of Finance, Oscar Coine: We need to create a link between our AP system and Invoicing. That must be our number one priority! We need to get into the 21st century. Let's get financial software like everyone else has. From the VP if Logistics, Peter Piquer: Although orders are sent to us immediately, we still have to manually create pick tickets to fill the order. These are actual pieces of paper. Do you believe that?! Getting everything online has to be our number one priority! We need to get into the 20th century. Let's get logistics software like everyone else has.

context: ABC Transportation is a growing company specializing in shipping and trucking of consumer and industrial goods. They have recently expanded and have been experiencing issues with their rapid growth. The biggest problem for them has been their inability to invoice customers quickly, which in turn slows their revenue and causes occasional cash flow issues. The problem is compounded by issues related to the integration of two recently acquired shipping companies into their main finance and accounting system. The separate systems vary in complexity and sophistication. For example, their most recent and largest acquisition, Overland Trucking, has antiquated systems and processes. It uses paper forms for: recording drivers' hours, noting packing charges, and documenting insurance coverage. The company would prefer the systems be integrated. Root cause analysis has revealed a reluctance among drivers and customers to drop the paper forms, since drivers can easily see the items loaded onto their trucks and customers can view and approve the shipments quickly. However, the forms are occasionally lost, which can result in billing delays of one to two days. The additional work to reconstruct missing forms occupies the bookkeeping staff, delaying both the routing and exception shipments. ABC is routinely 2-3 weeks late with invoices as a result. Some other related issues for the Overland systems include: 1. Local Shipments: The paper forms are brought back to the company offices the evening of the move. 2. Non-local Shipments: The paper forms are mailed back to the company offices the day after a move is completed. This process can delay receipt of required info by up to a week. Overland tried faxing the forms into Accounting for all non-local moves, but it was unreliable due to unavailability of fax machines in motels where crews stayed. 3. Overland waits until all expenses are received before they can invoice. 4. 3rd-party providers: if a third party is involved in packing or loading shipments, Overland waits until the third party invoices them before they can invoice the customer. It is more efficient to use this method, but it can add an additional 1-2 weeks to the time needed to complete the invoicing, depending on how quickly the 3rd party submits their invoice. 5. Overland provides paper-only copies of bills and sends them in duplicate to customers. The process requires a copy be returned to Accounting to match payments against the proper invoice and account. Many customers prefer to pay invoices electronically, and are frustrated. Overland also spends time matching payments when they come in without invoices to the applicable invoice, further delaying the invoicing process. You have been assigned as a senior BA to help ABC improve its processes. The sponsor of the initiative has appointed a member of ABC and one from each of the new subsidiaries to assist you with your work. They are all interested in improving the business, especially in integrating the three companies.
question_text: Which of the following techniques would lead to the greatest initial value for ABC Transportation and why?

context: ABC Transportation is a growing company specializing in shipping and trucking of consumer and industrial goods. They have recently expanded and have been experiencing issues with their rapid growth. The biggest problem for them has been their inability to invoice customers quickly, which in turn slows their revenue and causes occasional cash flow issues. The problem is compounded by issues related to the integration of two recently acquired shipping companies into their main finance and accounting system. The separate systems vary in complexity and sophistication. For example, their most recent and largest acquisition, Overland Trucking, has antiquated systems and processes. It uses paper forms for: recording drivers' hours, noting packing charges, and documenting insurance coverage. The company would prefer the systems be integrated. Root cause analysis has revealed a reluctance among drivers and customers to drop the paper forms, since drivers can easily see the items loaded onto their trucks and customers can view and approve the shipments quickly. However, the forms are occasionally lost, which can result in billing delays of one to two days. The additional work to reconstruct missing forms occupies the bookkeeping staff, delaying both the routing and exception shipments. ABC is routinely 2-3 weeks late with invoices as a result. Some other related issues for the Overland systems include: 1. Local Shipments: The paper forms are brought back to the company offices the evening of the move. 2. Non-local Shipments: The paper forms are mailed back to the company offices the day after a move is completed. This process can delay receipt of required info by up to a week. Overland tried faxing the forms into Accounting for all non-local moves, but it was unreliable due to unavailability of fax machines in motels where crews stayed. 3. Overland waits until all expenses are received before they can invoice. 4. 3rd-party providers: if a third party is involved in packing or loading shipments, Overland waits until the third party invoices them before they can invoice the customer. It is more efficient to use this method, but it can add an additional 1-2 weeks to the time needed to complete the invoicing, depending on how quickly the 3rd party submits their invoice. 5. Overland provides paper-only copies of bills and sends them in duplicate to customers. The process requires a copy be returned to Accounting to match payments against the proper invoice and account. Many customers prefer to pay invoices electronically, and are frustrated. Overland also spends time matching payments when they come in without invoices to the applicable invoice, further delaying the invoicing process. You have been assigned as a senior BA to help ABC improve its processes. The sponsor of the initiative has appointed a member of ABC and one from each of the new subsidiaries to assist you with your work. They are all interested in improving the business, especially in integrating the three companies.

context: Overview Pied Piper Pizza Pie is located in a large metropolitan city. It is a privately owned business. The pizza shop has been open for business for the last thirteen years. It offers a variety of different types of pizza (deep dish, thin crust, specialty pizzas), with a variety of flavorful, colorful, and fresh ingredients for toppings. The mix of revenue comes from 30% delivery of pizza and 70% table service. They also receive 95% of their delivery orders through a web-based application. The owners were thinking about expanding to a second location, but sales have dropped off the last six months, especially on Friday and Saturday nights. These evenings represent the majority of the sales for the week. If sales for Friday and Saturday nights could pick up, then the company could be in a position to expand to a second location. Currently, Pied Piper Pizza Pie employs cooks, wait staff, delivery drivers, and some bus staff/dishwashers. Customers The customers may be either individuals or families. They currently have about 5,000 customers, but they market to an area populated with over 1 million people. Recognizing that pizza is a consumable product that they can re-sell to happy customers, they keep customers in their files forever, unless someone explicitly asks to be removed. Orders Orders are received: Through an online web application. When customers place a local phone call. Whoever is cooking, answers the one phone, writes down the order and starts cooking the pizza. When customers walk into the restaurant and place an order to be assembled at the shop and cooked later at home. The drivers are expected to take these walk-in orders and pass them to the cooks. For those customers that are dining in, the wait staff takes the order and the cooks usually bring the pizza out to the tables. Preparing and Cooking Each pizza is individually prepared and cooked in one of the four commercial convection ovens. Each cook is responsible for cooking their own pizza orders. The cooks are paid according to how many pizzas they can cook during their shift. From One of the Owners, Tony Piede: "We are not a small business anymore. We need technology to help us get to the next level. We need a computer system that processes the orders, including inventory replenishment. I would like to see us totally automate the business." From the Other Owner, Kiki Piede: "We need to re-think how we do business. Our processes are confusing to everyone. Paying cooks by the pizza creates conflicts. Having drivers take orders for takeout results in errors. We need new ovens. Our customers expect a wood-fired oven for specialty pizzas. I know it's expensive, but. From One of the Cooks: "What we need is more ovens. How can I cook any faster when I don't have enough oven space? I could cook more if they would expand the kitchen. We don't need any fancy computers, just more ovens!" Your role You are a BA consultant hired by the owners to maximize the value of their business.
question_text: Which technique would be most useful in helping Pied Piper Pizza determine their business need?

Silvia is a senior BA and part of a team assigned the task to evaluate changes to an inventory management system for Zammond Retailing. The current system has not been effective enough at providing automatic replenishment levels for key products. The major issues with the current system include: 
1. Popular products are frequently at low stock levels. 
2. Customer complaints have risen by 20% in the last year that their favorite products are not available. 
3. Less popular products are over-stocked, often resulting in mark-downs to sell them when the inventory needs to be turned over. 
The existing system relies on a combination of customer purchasing data and human analysis of industry trends and buyer behavior. The purchasing data is historical and succeeds in predicting future purchasing trends for approximately 80% of the company’s products. Silvia and the team discovered the company is satisfied with inventory levels for the products in the 80% category. 
The remaining 20% of the products are where Zammond wants the improvements. Approximately half of those are popular products that are understocked and half are the ones that are over-stocked. The chief buyer wants to concentrate on improvements to better predict what products are the most popular so the company can sell more. The head of distribution wants the team to reduce over-stocked products and save costs. 
After a great deal of study, the team is proposing an artificial intelligence component be added to the inventory management system. It would add a business intelligence structure to monitor consumer trends and import data to help predict future inventory demand. The biggest challenge the team sees is they have to work on optimizing popular products separately from optimizing the less popular ones. As a result the chief buyer and head of distribution are both contending their needs are the most important. 
The team has determined the cost to design and develop the entire predictive component is projected to be $200,000 and the cost to install it is $75,000. The changes are estimated to earn $80,000 the first year, $110,000 in year 2, and $180,000 in year 3.
question_text: Before recommending a change to the inventory system, what should the team have done?

context: Hourglass Consulting Hourglass Consulting places project management and business analysis consultants throughout Europe. It is headquartered in London and has built its reputation on providing project managers who know how to complete their projects on time and within budget. Recently they have hired business analysts (BAs) to their group of consultants. Sofsys. Hourglass was recently purchased by Sofsys, which is a global consulting firm based in Amsterdam, Netherlands. This new parent company expects every consultant to be "full-stack," that is, they are expected to be able to walk into any situation and work on any type of project (from Waterfall to Agile), to manage the project, to elicit and document requirements, to develop the requirements and user stories, to prioritize requirements, to be involved with the designs, to do quick SQL programs, to develop test cases, acceptance criteria, and to test the requirements all while bringing the project in on time. Bon Voyage. Your customer, Bon Voyage, specializes in supplying all manor of clothes and gear for travel, including such things clothes, handbags and suitcases, accessories, backpacks, etc. They are based in Paris, France and have been in business for over 50 years, employing 350 people. Bon Voyage hired Hourglass Consulting to create a new sales promotion system that interfaces with the Order system, the Distribution Center system, and the Retail Store system so that different items can be sold at different retail stores. The project is expected to have a consulting team a project manager, you, the BA, five application developers who look at such things the database conversion requirements, building "bridges" between the old and new software that will be removed once the new software is up and running, creating any user instructions not provided by the vendor, and running a parallel test between the old and new system to ensure the new system is operational before removing the old one. You and the key business stakeholder, Daniel Chardonne, will provide the training on the new system. There are no other business analysts or business stakeholders assigned. Key Roles The sponsor for the project, Albert Tournier, is the VP of Operations at Bon Voyage, who hired your consulting firm to manage this project. The key business stakeholder is Daniel Chardonne, is an experienced Buyer with the travel company. From the Sponsor, Albert Tournier We need to buy a software package. I've been talking to Logiciel de Voyage. They see no reason why we cannot get the new software up and running in 6 months. They said that they've done this hundreds of times before. If they have any questions, they can talk to Daniel. Let's just get going! From your new boss at Sofsys: We need to get the project done in 6 months. We have a lot to lose here. We've worked with Albert Tournier before and he says that if we don't deliver, he assures me that he will find someone who will. Revenues have not been great this year and we need this business. From the business stakeholder representative, Daniel Chardonne. Seems like a good project to me. I'm sure the software will work fine. Honestly, I don't have time right now for this project. I'm very busy. This is the season where we buy our new merchandise. I'll be more available in about three months. Until then we'll have to rely on the software company.
question_text: How are these requirements classified in the Requirements Classification Schema: converting the database, building "bridges" between the old and new software, creating user instructions, and running parallel tests? "

context:
New Mills Foods - Overview 
New Mills Foods is a specialty maker of food products, focusing mainly on grain-based items. The company started by processing grain into flour. New Mills later discovered they could use their raw ingredients to make their own food items such as cereal, energy bars, and cake mixes. They still sell flour, but the profit margin for that is lower than for finished products.
The company has experienced increasing competition from lower-cost suppliers of grain, which affects all of those products. In addition, consumers have slowly been moving away from grain-based breakfast products to things like yogurt, eggs, and protein shakes. Searching for ways to increase profits by reducing costs, the firm has launched an initiative to improve its production and shipping processes and assembled a team on which you are a BA. 
Below is a summary of what has been discovered in the effort so far. 
Processes for Producing Ready-to-Bake Products 
Once an order is received from the warehouse, it is transmitted instantly to the food production department. A copy of the order prints out and a record is added to the production database. New Mill uses a concept they call “work crews” to assemble products. Each crew has a “crew chief” to oversee that crew’s work and its output and 3-4 crew members to mix, assemble, and package the products. A few of their factories have experimented with robots for package assembly with mixed results. 
One member of the crew operates the input machinery and dials in the needed ingredients needed for the product. Another one makes sure all needed supplies are present (inner and outer packaging and glue). Another crew member loads cellophane into a machine that makes bags for holding the finished product. An additional crew member loads pre-printed box materials into a packaging machine. 
After product ingredients are mixed, they move by conveyer belt to be poured into the cellophane bags. The machinery seals the bags and moves them by conveyer to another station where a crew member oversees the bags getting placed into boxes, which are then sealed with glue. The boxes move by conveyer to a station where they are loaded into shipping boxes 24 in a box. 
Occasionally some boxes don't assemble properly as they are filled. If that happens, a crew member pulls the products off the conveyer and puts them in a problem bin. A “runner” comes by periodically to retrieve the bins and dispose of the problem products. More often than not, the problem boxes are thrown away due to lack of staff to resolve the issues. 
Process for Shipping Products 
As completed boxes are filled, crews place them on pallets to be delivered to the shipping department. Once a pallet is filled, a crew member moves it to the shipping area. Given production volumes, this movement of pallets occurs dozens of times a day. Once in the shipping area, a shipping clerk matches the pallets with bills of lading. Those are documents that will assign the pallets to appropriate trucks for shipping. If no bill of lading is available, the pallets remain stacked in the shipping area, sometimes for days at a time.

context:
Overview
City to Country (CTC) Realty is a growing realty company that focuses on business properties outside of major metropolitan areas. It specializes in matching up buyers and sellers of hobby farms, lake resorts, bed & breakfasts and other types of small businesses located in or around mid to small size towns.
CTC Realty has been in business for several years and has achieved significant growth while interest rates have been low. However, they know that low interest rates will not always remain, and they want to leverage technology to compete against larger commercial and residential realty companies.
Customers
CTC Realty has two types of customers spread throughout North America: buyers and sellers. Sellers include those who are often downsizing after raising a family or retiring and want something smaller and easier to manage. Buyers could be any person or business. 
Buyers and sellers pay for the services utilized, such as contractual assistance, realtor assistance and then pay the appropriate fees to the lenders. CTC receives 1% of the total selling price of the property at closing.
Business Goals, Objectives, and Requirements
CTC Realty would like to grow substantially to be able to compete with larger national firms. They want to increase their market share by 15% in the next year and decrease the time for their sale and purchase process from an average of 20 days down to 10 days. 
CTC Realty has determined that a self-service website will help them meet these goals and objectives by providing a communication vehicle for buyers and sellers of real estate to meet and share information. The applications will support all aspects of a real estate sale and also supply advice and recommendations. This includes connecting lenders, legal analysts, and financial analysts with buyers and sellers. The various parties will not need to meet face to face, but will be connected virtually through the app. 
For brevity we list only the Selling and Security processes. 
Selling Process
The web site will offer the ability for a seller to complete the entire property sales process. When a person is ready to sell their property, they will be able to go on-line to make the necessary arrangements. 
The seller may choose to select a realtor to help them through the sales process, represent themselves and have legal counsel create the appropriate documents, or use the standard document templates found on the website. The seller can list their property on line by selecting the level of service they desire entering the property specifications and information. The system will then provide a final confirmation number and make the property available for buyers to peruse. 
The seller then waits for an offer. The system will notify both the seller and the seller’s realtor (if applicable) of any offers. At this time, the seller or seller’s realtor may submit a counter offer.
Once the offer is accepted, the process moves to closing, which can be complex. All final documentation is created for the closing. The buyer and seller (and all selected support staff) review the contracts and agree to the sale. The buyer and seller request the system to assign pin numbers to electronically approve of all closing documents. The buyer and seller approve the closing contracts by entering their pin numbers. The system records the completion of the sale and registers the property in the buyer’s name. 
Security
Each user of the system will have a password and User ID. The user will use their email address as their user ID and create a unique 6-8 digit password. The system will also provide electronic pin numbers for the buyer, seller and appropriate realtors in order to electronically sign the e-documents.

context: Benedict's Moving and Transportation is an international carrier that provides residential, business, military/government, and international moving services. They offer related services such as relocation assistance, warehousing, and temporary and long-term storage. They have nine offices throughout North America. Benedict's is extremely interested in the quality of their moving and packing services, having earned an ISO 9001:2008 Certification. They have won other awards such as the last year's Hauling Excellence Award and the previous year's Superior Packing Award. Over 90% of their customers say they would use Benedict's again for their move. To help them stay focused on their core business and maintain their high quality, the company limits their offerings to primary moving and packing services and outsources specialty ones. For example, Benedict's performs basic packing of goods, but does not package large, specialty items like pool tables for home moving or industrial equipment for their commercial service. Instead they use third-party providers to pack up the specialty items. The income of Benedict's Moving has increased by 5-10% per year over the past several years. The past year or two, though, the company has been experiencing increasing cash flow problems. Their billing for services has been steadily getting later and later each year. The delays in billing mean their income is also delayed. Wanda Banks, the Chief Financial Officer at Benedict's, is concerned about the cash flow problem. "Our income has never been better yet we struggle each month with our cash flow. The first half of each month our cash is strained until our receipts start coming in. That has happened six out of the last nine months and has got to be fixed. "It's eating into our profits more and more. Once credit costs start increasing, it will be an even worse problem. I estimate that if we cut costs by just 10% across the board that we will fix this problem." Van Driver, the Vice President of Operations, is concerned about the CFO's cost-cutting idea. "There is no way we could absorb a 10% cut right now. Labor costs are rising and so are costs for boxes, packaging, and paper. My guys are out there working hard week in and week out. We didn't get those quality awards by cutting costs. "My idea is to give each crew chief an iPad to record hours and things, and have the customer sign electronically. Can't we just send the moving info in to Accounting that way?" The CIO of Benedict's is Surendra Patel, who is in favor of acquiring and installing a new ERP system like MoveSuite to help the company manage their entire process. He maintains there are several available packages with multiple components that would benefit the company. Surendra has researched one such package, whose advertising claims to offer these benefits to the company: Substantial, measurable increases in process efficiency through streamlined workflow Improved data integrity and consistency, reduced errors and multiple data instances Quicker learning curve through use of standard MS Windows interface Improved accountability through record ownership and event logging Transparent transaction visibility through detailed financial reporting "The system is expensive at $750,000, but is a worthwhile investment for us," Surendra adds.
question_text: The BA assigned to this effort wants to understand why the company needs to change before doing anything further. What does the BA need to do or obtain first?

context:
SailAway.com is a start-up company which will allow sailboat owners to offer their boats for rent or charter (the term commonly used in the boating industry). Boat owners all over the world will be able to list their boats for charter, and customers will be able to locate boats that meet their specifications and charter them. 
For boat owners, SailAway will provide the ability to list a boat for charter in a specific location, provide a description of the boat and its amenities, provide pictures of the boat, list charter rates by season, and optionally accept credit cards for deposits and payments through SailAway’s credit card handling services. SailAway will also provide optional boat insurance during the charter period. 
For customers wishing to charter a boat, SailAway will provide the ability to search for boats based on location, size, and amenities. Customers will be able to contact the owners, send deposits and payments by credit card (if the boat owner offers this service), and write a review of the boat after their charter completes. The site will also offer vacation insurance coverage.
If the site does well, SailAway plans to branch out and offer a sister site called MotorAway, a site that will offer charters of motor yachts. 
You are part of the Scrum Team formed for this project. Your Product Owner, Claire Buford, is from the product marketing department. You will be using Scrum, with which you have limited experience, and you have a newly certified Scrum master. 
Beginning four months from now, there will be a major marketing push to get boat owners to list their boats, and to make potential charter customers aware of the site. Claire needs to have at least a basic site up and running by that time.
From the Scrum Master:
“I talked to our project sponsor, the owner of SailAway. Basically what he’s looking for is a website up and running in four months. The website will connect boat owners with customers who want to rent boats. This will include international boat owners and customers. Boat owners will be able to list their boats, including descriptions and photos. Customers will indicate which boats they want and be able to pay. There will have to be some security features and penalties for boats that are returned damaged or not returned at all. 
“The sponsor wants the website fully functional when we go live. You know how sponsors are—they want everything and never want to pay for anything. The budget is going to be very important. We’re going to have to watch costs carefully. I’m thinking we’re going to need contract developers and they’re not cheap. I hope we get a team that can do testing. I think we’re going to need to purchase automated testing software. That’ll take a chunk out of the budget. Anyhow, let’s get the team assembled and get going right away!”
You are a business analyst assigned to the delivery team for the duration of the effort to create the initial Sail Away web site. Because Scrum is still somewhat new to your organization, several team members have voiced their concerns about such things as who has the authority and responsibility to propose changes to features and who ideally should be involved in the change discussions. Also of concern is who will analyze and approve any changes to priorities during the project. Given the organization’s past reliance on requirements documents, you also have an interest in how requirements and changes to them will be handled.
question_text: What is one of the first thing(s) you need to do to ensure a successful outcome?

context: ABC Transportation is a growing company specializing in shipping and trucking of consumer and industrial goods. They have recently expanded and have been experiencing issues with their rapid growth. The biggest problem for them has been their inability to invoice customers quickly, which in turn slows their revenue and causes occasional cash flow issues. The problem is compounded by issues related to the integration of two recently acquired shipping companies into their main finance and accounting system. The separate systems vary in complexity and sophistication. For example, their most recent and largest acquisition, Overland Trucking, has antiquated systems and processes. It uses paper forms for: recording drivers' hours, noting packing charges, and documenting insurance coverage. The company would prefer the systems be integrated. Root cause analysis has revealed a reluctance among drivers and customers to drop the paper forms, since drivers can easily see the items loaded onto their trucks and customers can view and approve the shipments quickly. However, the forms are occasionally lost, which can result in billing delays of one to two days. The additional work to reconstruct missing forms occupies the bookkeeping staff, delaying both the routing and exception shipments. ABC is routinely 2-3 weeks late with invoices as a result. Some other related issues for the Overland systems include: 1. Local Shipments: The paper forms are brought back to the company offices the evening of the move. 2. Non-local Shipments: The paper forms are mailed back to the company offices the day after a move is completed. This process can delay receipt of required info by up to a week. Overland tried faxing the forms into Accounting for all non-local moves, but it was unreliable due to unavailability of fax machines in motels where crews stayed. 3. Overland waits until all expenses are received before they can invoice. 4. 3rd-party providers: if a third party is involved in packing or loading shipments, Overland waits until the third party invoices them before they can invoice the customer. It is more efficient to use this method, but it can add an additional 1-2 weeks to the time needed to complete the invoicing, depending on how quickly the 3rd party submits their invoice. 5. Overland provides paper-only copies of bills and sends them in duplicate to customers. The process requires a copy be returned to Accounting to match payments against the proper invoice and account. Many customers prefer to pay invoices electronically, and are frustrated. Overland also spends time matching payments when they come in without invoices to the applicable invoice, further delaying the invoicing process. You have been assigned as a senior BA to help ABC improve its processes. The sponsor of the initiative has appointed a member of ABC and one from each of the new subsidiaries to assist you with your work. They are all interested in improving the business, especially in integrating the three companies.
question_text: Which of the following techniques would lead to the greatest initial value for ABC Transportation and why?

context: Overview Belle's Bags is a rapidly growing online seller of handbags and purses, wallets, and accessories. Belle began making handbags as a hobby to sell at local art fairs. They were so popular, that she decided to become an entrepreneur and started selling through the internet. She has become so successful that she employs hundreds of people, including many working at the distribution center (DC) 24/7. Belle's sells to individuals and wholesale to a number of other retailers. Customers may be either individuals or businesses. An individual can also be a business. For example, Marisa Fortes might buy a purse for herself and a variety of product for her retail business. Most of the business customers are boutique retailers. Orders are placed online. There is no limit to the number of line items that can be placed on an order. An order really isn't an order unless at least one item has been ordered. Each line item is associated with a specific order. The Order system checks immediately to see if the item is in stock in the Inventory system. If in stock, the items on the order are reserved and immediately transmitted to the DC, where they are filled and shipped. If any of the items on the order are out of stock, that item is placed in a status of "Open." When the open item(s) is received in the DC, the Order system is updated immediately and the item is filled. The status of each item is updated along the way in a variety of systems, including the Order, Inventory, Accounting, Picking, and Shipping systems. When a customer inquires about the status of an order online, they must enter the order number and then the system checks the status of each order item on the order in each of the systems where it is stored. Once found, the system displays the status of the item. When the customer calls about the status of an order item, the CSR’s must check each system individually. From the CEO, Belle Bright: We're experiencing tremendous growing pains and our slow systems are not keeping up. We do care about customer service. The number of complaints has increased by about 10% over the last two years. Some of the comments on social media can be quite nasty. From the customer service supervisor, Dawn Murphy: Boy do we need new order processing software around here. We have a lot of customer complaints. For example, when customers would like to check their order status, it takes forever because the order system must request information from multiple systems, some of which are very slow. Sigh. From the VP of Finance, Oscar Coine: We need to create a link between our AP system and Invoicing. That must be our number one priority! We need to get into the 21st century. Let's get financial software like everyone else has. From the VP if Logistics, Peter Piquer: Although orders are sent to us immediately, we still have to manually create pick tickets to fill the order. These are actual pieces of paper. Do you believe that?! Getting everything online has to be our number one priority! We need to get into the 20th century. Let's get logistics software like everyone else has.
question_text: Which of these represents the best statement of business need?

context:
Overview
Newbank is a mid-sized regional bank that has had steady but not exceptional growth in the last decade. During this time it concentrated on expanding into new national markets and upgrading its computer systems. More recently, it has seen a sharp rise in customer complaints concerning the amount of paper that is generated and sent to customers, including statements, promotions, tax forms, and other bank communications. 
Current System Limitations
The business case for the initiative cited these major business problems to be addressed: 
• Currently statements can be sent to only one address, and they are addressed to the customer who is designated as the primary customer on the account. This lack of flexibility has been one of the chief sources of customer complaints. 
• Numerous requests have been received to have duplicate statements sent to different customers at different locations. 
• In all of its current systems (checking, savings, loans, etc.), each account at Newbank keeps its own name/address information, which means the same customer information is entered and maintained several times, once for each account. 
• Newbank must separately mail or email monthly statements, end-of-year interest reports, and other official mailings. This issue costs the bank excess postage and other costs, and is annoying to customers. 
The proposed solution for the initiative included consolidating address information into one place for all of a customer’s accounts. It also proposed adding the option of receiving one comprehensive statement for all the accounts in one household but also to send separate statements to account owners who request it. These features gives them a competitive advantage. 
The following information related to handling statements was available for you to review, the first three of which are shortened for brevity: 
CUSTOMERS - 
Newbank has several types of customers, including consumers, commercial, loan, and mortgage. The majority of their customers are consumers, but low interest rates have helped the loan and mortgage business as well. The bank is hoping to increase its commercial customers by 10-15% this year.
ACCOUNTS - 
An account is a vehicle for customers to safely keep money in a financial institution for some purpose, such as: a) the ability to write checks and have them be paid by the bank, b) to accumulate interest and c) to have liquidity of funds. Newbank offers checking, savings, and certificates of deposit.
LOCATIONS - 
Customers may have any number of addresses. Newbank indicates a primary address as a default, but that default can be changed upon customer request, either written or through the website. 
STATEMENTS
A. BUSINESS DEFINITIONS 
• A statement is a document that lists a) details of transactions that occurred during the most recent reporting period and b) summary information, such as totals of transaction types and beginning/ending balances. 
• Synonyms: None
B. BUSINESS RULES 
• An account is reported on at least one statement for free. If a customer desires additional reporting of an account, they can have it done for a fee (defined elsewhere). 
• A statement reports at least one account. 
• A statement is mailed to at least one location. 
• A location can receive several statements. 
C. EXAMPLES 
• Statement A: mailed to home address - Account 501 - joint checking between Bob and Mary No fee, Account 502 - individual checking - Bob No fee 
• Statement B: mailed to Bob's office - Account 502 - individual checking - Bob Fee 
• Statement C: both mailed to Mary's office - Account 504 - individual savings - Mary No fee, Account 504 - duplicate copy Fee

context: DC Computers is a large provider of computers and related equipment. Back in the 1980s they started out in the garage of their founder, David Collins. They made their first computers from spare parts found at rummage sales and surplus houses. Today, they import huge quantities of parts from around the world. They have production facilities in their Mesa, AZ headquarters as well as in China and Viet Nam. The hallmarks of DC Computers have been customization and customer service. They make computers to order, and they do it quickly and for competitive cost. They are not the lowest cost provider, but they excel at giving customers the greatest number of options of any computer maker and they succeed at going out of their way to please customers. A large share of employees has been with the firm since its start, and they are extremely knowledgeable. DC's products and service results in over 90% customer retention rate. Lately though, mergers in the industry, such as between Dell and EMC, have increased competition. Apple and Toshiba have steadily and gradually increased their market share. DC has not increased their market share, but at least has held it constant. Forecasts show, though, they will lose 5% of their market share in the next year if they don't make some improvements. The executive team at DC Computers has stated an objective of increasing market share by 3% in the next year and 5% the following year. DC's own computer applications and systems are 10 years old, even though they use current hardware. They have a fairly new order entry system and provide basic accounting and inventory management, but the other applications they use independently developed or purchased over the years. DC has grown to a level where they have produced 500,000 products in the past 12 months between their three factories. They operate 360 days a year, slowing down only for major national holidays in the countries they operate in. Their Mesa factory is still the largest, producing 300,000 products the past year, followed by China with half that. Viet Nam is the newest plant, producing 50,000 products in the first 9 months it was open. The company expects it to grow the fastest. Because of their growth, DC's processes and training have not been kept up-to-date, and quality has begun to slip as of late. The company relies heavily on the knowledge of their experienced staff to produce their computers, with the experienced staff responsible for training the newer workers. An analysis of the current situation revealed that: * Products fail their post-production test at the rate of 10%. * Lower-skilled workers combined with insufficient training have increased errors by 31% over the last several years. * The company's return rate due to quality problems on new products has increased from .5% in 15 years ago to 1% 5 years ago to 3% last year. * Customer complaints that averaged below 5% of all calls for many years have jumped to 8 and 10% of all calls the last 2 years running, respectively. Most complaints have been due to slow delivery of products and products not assembled properly or with defective parts. Management blames its slower sales growth on the increased quality problems and wants to address them immediately. From David Collins, CEO David feels strongly that the company's problems are due to not having integrated processes. He went to a conference on Business Process Management and learned that processes need to be interconnected to succeed, especially core processes. "We are going backwards as a company! It used to be we could assemble a new product in a day, now it can take us several days or even longer!! We must have an Enterprise Resource Planning product like PAS to tie all everything together. The software will force us to improve and standardize our processes. From Robert Delupe, CIO DC's sales and order entry systems were overhauled last year, and the executives are satisfied they are working properly. They would like to fix as many systems as possible within the next year, to show the board of directors they are making improvements. Robert thinks the need for an ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system is overblown. He'd prefer to fix the current applications, and build new ones that may be needed. "We've invested thousands of dollars in building our current production scheduling, ordering, and shipping systems. Our developers and DBAs understand it extremely well, and we can fix problems really quickly when they are reported. It would be a shame to throw that all away. Plus, there will be a huge learning curve with PAS - I've heard from colleagues who have installed it that it's a really transition." You have been assigned as a senior business analyst to this initiative.
question_text: The CEO questions whether the current manufacturing and related systems can help the company gain new market share. He wants the team to obtain a popular ERP software package called PAS. What is the first thing you should do?

context: DC Computers is a large provider of computers and related equipment. Back in the 1980s they started out in the garage of their founder, David Collins. They made their first computers from spare parts found at rummage sales and surplus houses. Today, they import huge quantities of parts from around the world. They have production facilities in their Mesa, AZ headquarters as well as in China and Viet Nam. The hallmarks of DC Computers have been customization and customer service. They make computers to order, and they do it quickly and for competitive cost. They are not the lowest cost provider, but they excel at giving customers the greatest number of options of any computer maker and they succeed at going out of their way to please customers. A large share of employees has been with the firm since its start, and they are extremely knowledgeable. DC's products and service results in over 90% customer retention rate. Lately though, mergers in the industry, such as between Dell and EMC, have increased competition. Apple and Toshiba have steadily and gradually increased their market share. DC has not increased their market share, but at least has held it constant. Forecasts show, though, they will lose 5% of their market share in the next year if they don't make some improvements. The executive team at DC Computers has stated an objective of increasing market share by 3% in the next year and 5% the following year. DC's own computer applications and systems are 10 years old, even though they use current hardware. They have a fairly new order entry system and provide basic accounting and inventory management, but the other applications they use independently developed or purchased over the years. DC has grown to a level where they have produced 500,000 products in the past 12 months between their three factories. They operate 360 days a year, slowing down only for major national holidays in the countries they operate in. Their Mesa factory is still the largest, producing 300,000 products the past year, followed by China with half that. Viet Nam is the newest plant, producing 50,000 products in the first 9 months it was open. The company expects it to grow the fastest. Because of their growth, DC's processes and training have not been kept up-to-date, and quality has begun to slip as of late. The company relies heavily on the knowledge of their experienced staff to produce their computers, with the experienced staff responsible for training the newer workers. An analysis of the current situation revealed that: * Products fail their post-production test at the rate of 10%. * Lower-skilled workers combined with insufficient training have increased errors by 31% over the last several years. * The company's return rate due to quality problems on new products has increased from .5% in 15 years ago to 1% 5 years ago to 3% last year. * Customer complaints that averaged below 5% of all calls for many years have jumped to 8 and 10% of all calls the last 2 years running, respectively. Most complaints have been due to slow delivery of products and products not assembled properly or with defective parts. Management blames its slower sales growth on the increased quality problems and wants to address them immediately. From David Collins, CEO David feels strongly that the company's problems are due to not having integrated processes. He went to a conference on Business Process Management and learned that processes need to be interconnected to succeed, especially core processes. "We are going backwards as a company! It used to be we could assemble a new product in a day, now it can take us several days or even longer!! We must have an Enterprise Resource Planning product like PAS to tie all everything together. The software will force us to improve and standardize our processes. From Robert Delupe, CIO DC's sales and order entry systems were overhauled last year, and the executives are satisfied they are working properly. They would like to fix as many systems as possible within the next year, to show the board of directors they are making improvements. Robert thinks the need for an ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system is overblown. He'd prefer to fix the current applications, and build new ones that may be needed. "We've invested thousands of dollars in building our current production scheduling, ordering, and shipping systems. Our developers and DBAs understand it extremely well, and we can fix problems really quickly when they are reported. It would be a shame to throw that all away. Plus, there will be a huge learning curve with PAS - I've heard from colleagues who have installed it that it's a really transition." You have been assigned as a senior business analyst to this initiative.

context: Overview Ben's Bikes is a rapidly growing seller of bicycles, parts, and accessories in the United States and Canada. We currently have one large retail outlet adjacent to our distribution center, and we wholesale to a number of other retailers. Our customers may be either individuals or businesses. An individual can be a member of a household. A business can be a bike shop with a few branches, or a large chain of a hundred or more franchised shops. Each business customer has to be set up with a contact who can be reached for questions. Each customer can have many contacts, but each contact is for only one customer. For example, Anna is a contact at one location, Joanna at another location for the same customer, and Tomas at a third location for the same customer. In another example, Clara works part-time at one location and part-time at another location for the same customer. The contacts can work out of one location or many. And the locations can house one contact or many. For example, the contact Clara works part-time at one branch location and part-time at another location for the same customer, Best Bikes. In another example, a branch location for Best Bikes has designated two contacts, Erlich and Brenda. The current Order Processing system requires that an address or location is set up at the same time a customer is established. Individuals can have orders sent to multiple locations. For example Tomek can order a helmet and have it sent to his home, a bike to his daughter away at college, and a case of energy bars sent to his office. Businesses can have a headquarters location and/or retail store locations. Ben's Bikes distribution center is a separate location. We will ship as much of an order together as possible within 24 hours. If we don't have the entire quantity of a line item, we wait until the entire quantity has been received before processing the order. A shipment to a large corporate customer may include multiple orders. A large order might require several shipments. The contents of each shipment are recorded using the on-line system. From the CEO, Ben MacinTyre: We need to reduce our cycle time from the time a customer places an order until it's out the door. It seems to take an average of 72 hours these days, but our competitor, Mike's Bikes advertises on their website that they can ship products in 24 hours. And they have a money-back guarantee. We rarely seem to meet our 24-hour cutoff times. I'm concerned about our image with customers. If we can't get our orders out sooner, I'm afraid customers will go to our competitors. I figure that we might lose revenues of about two million dollars this year because of our slow systems. From the VP of Marketing, Douglas Colby: Our expenses are too high. Some of it is the paper catalog, which is very expensive to produce. We thought it was a good idea to produce multiple catalogs, one featuring women and one featuring men. But not only has the cost become prohibitive, but more often than not the paper catalogs are probably tossed since most customers order online. From the VP if IT, Hector Boyle: I'm not sure we have the infrastructure to support our rapid growth. Ben wants a quicker system and better reporting, but we need new databases and business analytics software. Before anything else, though, we really need to update our infrastructure.
question_text: Which of these represents the best statement of business need?

context: Four companies will be involved in this project. Bridgeworks is based in Paris, France and designs and builds bridges all over the world. There is a contractual commitment made by Megaworks as part of its purchase of Bridgeworks to install a suite of commercial software packages. Megaworks has contacted your consulting company, Accusys, to purchase and install the new software. In addition to commercial software, Bridgeworks wants a web-based custom developed application that will integrate with the purchased software package. AccuSys is an international consulting company hired by Megaworks to implement commercial software for Bridgeworks. Megaworks hired your consulting firm to manage this project because they didn't think Bridgeworks had the skills implement this kind of a project. Software vendor. Has not yet been selected. Key Players. You are a business analyst who has been assigned to help install commercial software for Bridgeworks. Your role is to select a package, configure/customize the package as appropriate, coordinate the user acceptance testing within the five departments, and train the five departments on the new processes and application user interfaces. These five departments are known for "not working well together" but will need to because there are interdepartmental dependencies. You only have six months to get your work done. The end date of the project is not negotiable because of the contractual agreement between Megaworks and Bridgeworks. Sponsor. The sponsor for the project, Tomas Gunther, is the VP of Operations. Tomas can't imagine why the project would take more than 6 months. He has said, "I was really expecting to have the software suite selected by now. The date absolutely cannot change, as the fines for breaking the contract are significant. Key business stakeholder. Henri LaBove, is an experienced Bridgeworks' construction manager. You are the assigned to work with Henri and to ensure that there is a smooth implementation of the new suite of commercial software. You are expected to work very closely with Henri and the stakeholders he has selected in the five affected departments. Henri has said, "This acquisition has been extremely disruptive to Bridgeworks. Our employees are overworked, underpaid, and just plain scared about keeping their jobs. We've been around for a lot longer than our new parent company, Megaworks. What do they know about building and maintaining bridges, anyway? Their execs have been swarming around here, asking questions about our business, our processes, our systems. I wish they'd just leave us alone and let us get our jobs done. Your boss, the project manager. Suzanne Johansson also works for AccuSys. She is confident that she can get the project done within the timeframe required. She has said, "I enjoy working on global projects. I'm sure you can figure out the requirements details. We might have to skip user acceptance testing ourselves and let the vendor handle that due to the time constraints.

context:
Silvia is a senior BA and part of a team assigned the task to evaluate changes to an inventory management system for Zammond Retailing. The current system has not been effective enough at providing automatic replenishment levels for key products. The major issues with the current system include: 
1. Popular products are frequently at low stock levels. 
2. Customer complaints have risen by 20% in the last year that their favorite products are not available. 
3. Less popular products are over-stocked, often resulting in mark-downs to sell them when the inventory needs to be turned over. 
The existing system relies on a combination of customer purchasing data and human analysis of industry trends and buyer behavior. The purchasing data is historical and succeeds in predicting future purchasing trends for approximately 80% of the company’s products. Silvia and the team discovered the company is satisfied with inventory levels for the products in the 80% category. 
The remaining 20% of the products are where Zammond wants the improvements. Approximately half of those are popular products that are understocked and half are the ones that are over-stocked. The chief buyer wants to concentrate on improvements to better predict what products are the most popular so the company can sell more. The head of distribution wants the team to reduce over-stocked products and save costs. 
After a great deal of study, the team is proposing an artificial intelligence component be added to the inventory management system. It would add a business intelligence structure to monitor consumer trends and import data to help predict future inventory demand. The biggest challenge the team sees is they have to work on optimizing popular products separately from optimizing the less popular ones. As a result the chief buyer and head of distribution are both contending their needs are the most important. 
The team has determined the cost to design and develop the entire predictive component is projected to be $200,000 and the cost to install it is $75,000. The changes are estimated to earn $80,000 the first year, $110,000 in year 2, and $180,000 in year 3.
question_text: Before recommending a change to the inventory system, what should the team have done?

context: Overview Pied Piper Pizza Pie is located in a large metropolitan city. It is a privately owned business. The pizza shop has been open for business for the last thirteen years. It offers a variety of different types of pizza (deep dish, thin crust, specialty pizzas), with a variety of flavorful, colorful, and fresh ingredients for toppings. The mix of revenue comes from 30% delivery of pizza and 70% table service. They also receive 95% of their delivery orders through a web-based application. The owners were thinking about expanding to a second location, but sales have dropped off the last six months, especially on Friday and Saturday nights. These evenings represent the majority of the sales for the week. If sales for Friday and Saturday nights could pick up, then the company could be in a position to expand to a second location. Currently, Pied Piper Pizza Pie employs cooks, wait staff, delivery drivers, and some bus staff/dishwashers. Customers The customers may be either individuals or families. They currently have about 5,000 customers, but they market to an area populated with over 1 million people. Recognizing that pizza is a consumable product that they can re-sell to happy customers, they keep customers in their files forever, unless someone explicitly asks to be removed. Orders Orders are received: Through an online web application. When customers place a local phone call. Whoever is cooking, answers the one phone, writes down the order and starts cooking the pizza. When customers walk into the restaurant and place an order to be assembled at the shop and cooked later at home. The drivers are expected to take these walk-in orders and pass them to the cooks. For those customers that are dining in, the wait staff takes the order and the cooks usually bring the pizza out to the tables. Preparing and Cooking Each pizza is individually prepared and cooked in one of the four commercial convection ovens. Each cook is responsible for cooking their own pizza orders. The cooks are paid according to how many pizzas they can cook during their shift. From One of the Owners, Tony Piede: "We are not a small business anymore. We need technology to help us get to the next level. We need a computer system that processes the orders, including inventory replenishment. I would like to see us totally automate the business." From the Other Owner, Kiki Piede: "We need to re-think how we do business. Our processes are confusing to everyone. Paying cooks by the pizza creates conflicts. Having drivers take orders for takeout results in errors. We need new ovens. Our customers expect a wood-fired oven for specialty pizzas. I know it's expensive, but. From One of the Cooks: "What we need is more ovens. How can I cook any faster when I don't have enough oven space? I could cook more if they would expand the kitchen. We don't need any fancy computers, just more ovens!" Your role You are a BA consultant hired by the owners to maximize the value of their business.
question_text: Which technique would be most useful in helping Pied Piper Pizza determine their business need?
